content:
Hi all. I do some experiments on kernel rust code as below. Unexpectedly, this nonsense change ends up compiling :confused:! Could anyone explain what's going on here?
P.S. I am pretty sure that compiler is taking these code. 

diff --git a/rust/kernel/device.rs b/rust/kernel/device.rs
index dea06b79ecb5..b96f8e742fe1 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/device.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/device.rs
@@ -555,5 +555,5 @@ macro_rules! dev_info {
 /// ```
 #[macro_export]
 macro_rules! dev_dbg {
-    ($($f:tt)*) => { $crate::dev_printk!(pr_dbg, $($f)*); }
+    ($($f:tt)*) => { $crate::dev_printk!(pr_dbg_fake, $($g)*); }
 }
If dev_dbg is not used anywhere, then your change will not result in an error.
@Alice Ryhl you said "used" means runtime calling? I searched dev_dbg! in several source files.

Luc Ma said:

Alice Ryhl you said "used" means runtime calling? I searched dev_dbg! in several source files.

Sorry for the noise, i just found dev_dbg!'s actually never are used because i overlook RUST_FW_LOADER_ABSTRACTIONS causing the entire of nova_core not to be compiled at all.
Luc Ma has marked this topic as resolved.
